channel.title: poetry on Dillon de Silva
channel.description: Recent content in poetry on Dillon de Silva

item.title: Gifts
item.description: The beauty that resides within nature Is one of which provides a bird with wings A fish with gills And mechanics to a beaver Though you and I alike May not possess such roaring crafts The light whose hand at which we were bred Gifted each and everyone of us With a power unlike no other The neurons firing within the complexity of the mind Or the reverberation of our heart with song

item.title: isolated.
item.description: Heroes are forged in caves Within one’s deepest desolation The good witch’s brewery opens A cauldron to craft potions Ingredients of love and gratitude Wand to paper, light to nature Now as the devil reigns temporarily It is important to confine But never let your mind Fall short, six feet behind Though I seem somewhat clever My oasis is but a replica of yours Navigating survival amidst the new evolution

item.title: A Momentary Appreciation for Sound
item.description: If all the sounds in the world vanished amongst silence If the unique spectrum of tones were to diffuse away Oh the treachery For I cannot imagine such a world A world without sound Like the waves radiating through the oceans She travels far and wide The wild summer parties manifesting with adolescent romance The old couple sharing a slow dance Whilst the young ones learn to boogie Yet here I lay
